observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the:
Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer-Designate to reconcile their records of the PPE accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereto;
Further, for both officials to maintain complete PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, in accordance with the forms prescribed in Annexes 9 and 37 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II. Henceforth, conduct periodic reconciliation of their respective records, at least on a quarterly basis, to ensure accuracy of the PPE accounts.
Inventory Committee/Team to conduct a thorough and complete physical inventory of all the Municipality’s properties.  Thereafter, prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed form (Annex 49, NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II) and submit the same to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year.

We recommended that Management require the Accountant to determine all the property items recognized in the PPE accounts but did not meet the capitalization threshold and prepare the adjusting entries to reclassify them into the appropriate expense and government equity accounts. Henceforth, we recommended the use of proper inventory and expense accounts to record the receipt and issuance of semi-expendable items that did not meet the capitalization threshold, respectively.
We recommended that Management review, revise and update its 1992 Municipal Tax Code pursuant to Section 191 of the LGC of 1991.

We recommended that Management require the BAC, through BAC Secretariat, to comply with the posting requirements and submission to the GPPB of APP, its supplemental and PMRs, to ensure transparency and integrity in the procurement process.

We recommended that Management integrate the cost of implementing CSHP in the construction costs and comply with the minimum health and safety requirements in construction projects pursuant to the DOLE DO No. 13, s. of 1998 and other pertinent guidelines issued by DPWH.

We recommended that Management, require the BAC, to ensure the completeness of pertinent documents that shall form part of the Contract provided under Section 37.2.3. We also recommended to ensure that these documents be specifically indicated in the Bid Data Sheet and Contract Agreement.

We recommended that Management requires the BAC to strictly comply with the procurement guidelines and procedures prescribed in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184. We also recommend that the BAC and the Technical Working Group conduct thorough post-qualification procedures to ensure the integrity of the award of contract.

We recommended that Management require the personnel assigned in preparing the RFQ to accomplish said forms completely to comply with the Revised IRR and strengthen the internal control on procurement.

We recommended the Management require the concerned personnel to submit the government contracts, purchase orders and the supporting documents within five days after perfection pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that Management:
Require all the Departments involve in the implementation of its PRDP project to give their full attention and cooperation in the effective implementation of the proposed Construction of Municipal Slaughterhouse project at Brgy. Sta. Cruz; and
Require the concerned Barangay Officials to immediately submit the Liquidation Reports of the previously downloaded amount to them, to expedite the implementation of the eight identified barangay augmentation projects.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to record the DRRM Funds in the Special Trust Fund Books under the Trust Liability – DRRM account. Henceforth, we recommended the proper recording of receipt and utilization of DRRM Funds and completeness of information thereof in the prescribed reports in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management require the MDRRMO to plan/identify PPAs, which shall be incorporated in the LDRRMFIP, to utilize the various cash donations according to its specific purpose and to address the needs of the intended beneficiaries.

We recommended that Management through the LSB:
Ensure that the prescribed planning and budgeting process are strictly adhered to; and
Strictly observe the timelines in the planning and budgeting process as prescribed under the DepEd, DBM & DILG Joint Circular in order to optimize the utilization of the SEF budget and to ensure that the benefits are enjoyed by its beneficiaries in a timely manner.

We recommended that the LSB strictly observe the proper timelines in the preparation and submission of the required reports in its proper format and within the prescribed period to allow the Audit Team conduct a thorough verification thereof.

We recommended that Management through the Solid Waste Management Board properly implement the approved 10-year plan and to assess its effectiveness and relevance in relation to developments in the field of solid waste management.
Also, create a plantilla for the Municipal Environment and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to effectively oversee the duties and functions of the office.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
GAD Focal Person (a) to ensure that the programs, projects and activities included in the GAD Plan and Budget are only gender-related issues and concerns to ensure proper and effective utilization the GAD fund.
Budget Officer to charge the Assistance to Individuals/Families in Crisis Situation (AICS) in the appropriate fund.

We recommended that Management, through the GAD Focal Person, spearhead the updating and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as a more reliable basis for gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation.
Likewise, use the HGDG, PIMME and FIMME forms as tools in identifying PPAs attributed to GAD budget for efficiency in identifying the issues, analysis, and monitoring of its effectiveness.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the GAD Focal Person to adhere to the approved GPB in the implemention of GAD programs and activities.  In case of changes, Management should prepare a revised GPB for approval by the concerned officials and oversight agencies; and
Require concerned officers and employees to practice consistency and observe the guidelines in data gathering and reporting.  Make certain that the revised GPB is communicated to the concerned oversight bodies.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
Municipal Accountant to continue with the proper withholding of the correct premiums from employees’ salary;
Municipal Treasurer to remit the full amount that was withheld from employees’ salary, together with the government’s share in the employees’ premiums.
We also recommend the Municipal Government create plantilla positions for the Human Resource Management Office to effectively manage the employment process and also maintain and update the employee records.

We recommended that Management direct the Acting Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to immediately reconcile the Cash-in Local Treasury, Petty Cash Fund and Cash-in Bank-Local Currency, Current Accounts, and record the necessary adjustments.  Further, require all accountable officers to update their cashbooks daily;

Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant shall reconcile their books of accounts, at least quarterly, to avoid incurrence of accumulated unreconciled balances.

We commended Management, particularly the Municipal Accounting Office, for implementing our prior years’ audit recommendation to fully adhere to government rules and regulations on the liquidation of cash advances. We recommend that Management continue to ensure that government funds are safeguarded from wastage through the monitoring of cash advances granted.

Further, require the liquidation of the remaining unliquidated cash advances granted to the accountable officers of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a detailed analysis on the RPT/SET Receivable accounts and its related Deferred RPT/Deferred SET Income accounts and make the necessary adjustments to ensure the fair presentation of the accounts in the year-end financial statements.

We recommended that Management require:

The Municipal Treasurer to update the RPTAR /Taxpayer’s index card based on the Assessment Roll and furnish the Municipal Accounting Office the correct certified list of taxes due and collectible at the beginning of each year as basis in establishing the correct RPT/SET Receivables and the related Deferred RPT/Deferred SET Income accounts;

The Municipal Assessor to provide the Municipal Treasurer with the latest total assessment of the taxable properties as well as the taxes to be imposed before the 31st day of December of every year; and

The Municipal Accountant to review and reconcile the report before setting up the receivable account.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace and analyze the transactions and reconcile the difference of ₱215,485 in the reciprocal accounts Due to/Due from Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds as of December 31, 2020.

Management Comment:
The Municipal Treasurer agreed that the LSB indeed allocated more to the Sports Development than any other programs that could have been beneficial to almost all the pupils and students of the municipality. Being an “invitee” of the LSB during budgeting, the Municipal Budget Officer claimed to have provided the LSB technical inputs and copies of the latest issuances on budgeting and other related guidelines.
The School Investment Plans (SIPs) submitted by the DepEd Division Office lack necessary information to serve as inputs in the proper preparation of the LSB annual budget.  Further, the Division Education Development Plan (DEPD) and the National Expenditures Program (NEP) were not appropriately considered in the preparation of the LSB budget.
DepEd, DBM and DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 provides the following:
Section 4.0 Allowable Expenses Chargeable Against the SEF-
4.1 In all instances, the allocation for the following expenditure items chargeable against the SEF shall be net of the budgetary provision for the same or related item(s) in the budget for the DepEd and the ECCD Council, and that which may be funded out of the Special Purpose Funds.
Section 5.0 Planning and Budgeting for the SEF-
5.1The LSB shall formulate a three-year program indicating strategic prioritization policies in the allocating  of the SEF to schools taking into consideration equitable sharing, priority needs and such factors as enrollment ratio, distance of the schools, performance, drop-out rate, and location of schools, as well as the goals and objectives of the LGU’s Comprehensive Development Plan (CDP), and  the expenditures, programs, projects and activities in the  LGU’s Local Development Investment Program (LDIP) prior to the formulation and preparation of its  Annual Budget for the incoming year. It is understood that the DepEd representative to the LSB shall be responsible for coordinating municipal/city school plans with that of the province and ensuring that the School Improvement Plan (SIP) and the Division Education Development Plan (DEDP) are formulated collaboratively with the stakeholders in the community.
5.2.1All expenditure items to be included in the SEF Budget shall be among those provided under Section 4.0 hereof and contained in the approved SIP and DEDP.
Section5.3 The LSB shall consider the following, among others, in the preparation of the SEF Budget:
5.3.1 Recommendation of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the priorities indicated in the Schools Investment Plan (SIPs) and Division Education Development Plan (DEDPs) vis-à-vis the information from the DepEd Regional Office (RO) on the allocation of each school from the National Budget for the current year and the ensuing year as contained in the National Expenditure Program (NEP).
In our review of the LSB budget for 2019, we requested Management to furnish the Audit Team with a copy of DEDP and NEP of the DepEd Division Office to no avail.  We were only able to gather the Schools Improvement Plans (SIPs) in the custody of the Municipality.
The SIP consists of a Budget Matrix showing the sources of fund for its PPAs. We noticed, however, that the SIPs were not prepared in a consistent manner because while some PPAs have detailed cost estimates, the others have none.  Likewise, we noted the absence of the total fund allocation from DepEd Division Office/Regional Office that would show whether the funds received therefrom are sufficient or insufficient to allow the School Board to determine how much of the budget would be chargeable to the SEF.
Our review of one elementary school’s SIP showed that for Sports Development, the estimated lump sum amount ₱50,000 will come from solicitations, the SEF, PTA, and MOOE of the DepEd. The corresponding amounts to be raised from each source, however, were not indicated.  The absence of complete and accurate details from the SIPs such as the particular amount to be raised from each source is confusing and may result in overlapping source of funds such as the SEF and the DepEd budget.
In the preparation of the LSB annual budget, due consideration should be given to the various items identified in the SIPs since these are the actual needs of the different public schools.  Thus, priority should have been given thereto in the preparation of the SEF budget by the LSB in adherence to the DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require the LSB to formulate the required three-year strategic program.  Consider the recommendations of the DepEd PSDS and SDS as well as the SIPs as inputs in its preparation and furnish the Audit Team a copy thereof.
Henceforth, strictly adhere with the provisions of the Local Government Code and DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1 in the preparation of the School Board budget to ensure proper allocation of funds.
Further, we recommended that the School Board consider the following method in the preparation of SEF budget so that only the deficiency in the DepEd Division budget shall be provided funds therein:

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation for Management to:

Pursue the immediate approval of the Ten- Year SWMP as required under R.A. No. 9003 to ensure its sustainability, effectiveness and relevance in relation to local and international progress in the area of SWM; and

Intensify the SWM information dissemination campaign and enforce all regulations to the extent of imposing penalties for non-compliance.



We also recommended:

The immediate implementation of the safe closure and rehabilitation plan for the dumpsites located at Barangay Binitayan once the plan is approved by the Environmental Management Bureau; and

That Management expedite implementation of a garbage facility and the development and operation of a sanitary landfill as a final disposal site for solid and eventually residual wastes of the Municipality. To likewise consider the establishment of Common Waste Treatment and Disposal Facility. Section 44 of R.A. 9003 mandates municipalities among others to consolidate, or coordinate their efforts, services, and resources for purposes of jointly addressing common SWM problems and/or establishing common waste disposal facilities.

We also recommended that Management provide sufficient fund in 2019 for the implementation of the safe closure and rehabilitation of the two dumpsites.

The Municipal Treasurer to deposit all her collections intact daily or as necessary with the Authorized Government Depository Bank in accordance with COA Circular No. 92-382;

The Municipal Treasurer to refrain from using collections to pay cash advances or other disbursements which should be properly paid through checks; and

All concerned officers and employees to stop the practice of using cash advances other than the specific legal purpose for which it was granted in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002.

Further, the Municipal Treasurer shall record the disbursements aggregating to ₱0.49 million in the Cashbook-Cash in Bank. Furthermore, to record all check disbursements in the cashbook in numerical and chronological sequence, including cancelled checks.

We also recommended that Management stop the practice of delegating/designating collection functions to Municipal personnel who are not regular employees and not bonded to avoid losses resulting from misuse and other losses attributable to negligence in the keeping of such funds.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) instruct all concerned
to secure the documents to support the ownership of the real properties. Likewise, the
LCE to direct the Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory of its
PPE including the real properties and report the same in the RPCPPE

We also recommended for the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal
Accountant to exert extra effort to reconstruct pertinent real property records in order
that Subsidiary Ledgers/PLCs of real property owned by Municipality can be
established. Henceforth, transactions involving the acquisition and disposal of real
properties shall be recorded therein.

Further, the Property and Accounting Units shall conduct periodic
reconciliation of their records to ensure that the real properties are properly
recorded.

‘We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a. Instruct the Inventory Team to prepare the RPCPPE in the prescribed form
as shown in Annex 49 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I and
reconcile the inventory report with the Accounting records and any
discrepancies between the physical and book inventories must be
investigated, cleared and reconciled immediately, if necessary, written
explanations shall be required from persons responsible;

b. Instruct the Accounting Unit to reconcile immediately the PPE for any
discrepancies between the physical and book balances and adjust the noted
difference; and duly support any adjustments/corrections resulting from the
reconciliation with proper documentation; and

c. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain ledger cards for each
category of assets; and Real Property Ledger Cards for land that shall
contain the details of the PPE in the inventory control account in the General
Ledger cards and the Municipal Treasurer to maintain Property Cards for
PPE to account for the receipt and disposition of the same and to reconcile
with the ledger cards of the Accounting Unit and with other Property
records.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Management
require the Inventory Team to conduct complete physical inventory of the PPE,
prepare and submit completely accomplished RPCPPE to provide the Accounting Unit
adequate basis for the computation of depreciation. Likewise, the Accounting Unit is
required to compute the correct depreciation and make the necessary adjustment on
the affected accounts. Henceforth, depreciation must be computed for all depreciable
properties periodically to ensure the fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Treasurer and

the Municipal Accountant to look into the cause/s of discrepancy and
thereafter, make the necessary adjustments, as warranted. Henceforth, both should regularly reconcile
their records so that errors could be readily detected and adjusted to ensure reliability
of the Book balances reconciled with the Bank.

‘We recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

1. Prepare, update and submit the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements,
including the bank statements, paid checks and original debit/credit memos
and other supporting documents, on all depository accounts of the LGU on
a timely basis to ensure regular reconciliation of the balances of the accounts
and prompt recording of valid reconciling items, in accordance with
Sections 3.2 and 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011;

2. Effect the necessary adjusting entries for the prior years’ reconciling items
to assure the correctness of the Cash in Bank account in the financial
statements; and

3. Immediately record the cancellation and replacement of stale checks
amounting to P21,728.00 based from the List of Stale Checks from the
Treasurer following the pro-forma entries for stale checks provided in
Section 61 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume L.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to cause
the immediate submission of the 2016 year-end financial statements and schedules to
expedite the review and verification thereof by the Audit Team. Henceforth, for the
Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly Trial Balance, quarterly and
year-end FS with complete schedules and annexes within the prescribed timeframe in
accordance with the existing rules and regulations.

Further, we recommended that Management urgently take appropriate
measures to angment manpower complement of the Municipal Accounting office.

We recommended that Management:

1. Execute a Memorandum of Agreement with the supplier in the procurement
of goods for stockpiling in preparation for eventualities/calamities that may
occur in the future to avoid instances of issuing/distribution the relief goods
even in the absence of a calamity for fear that the goods shall be spoiled due
to the absence of a proper and adequate storeroom.

2. Strictly comply with laws, rules and regulations on the procurement of goods
intended for the victims of calamities.

‘We recommended that the Municipal Government return the PDAF balance
amounting to P185,006.82 to the Bureau of Treasury in accordance with the above cited
circular and the Supreme Court decision.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly comply with Section
10 of Annex E of Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of Republic Act No.
9184 in approving the request of contract time extension by the contractor. Further,
the Municipal Engineer should conduct investigation to validate the request and make
recommendation to the LCE.

We recommended that the LCE require immediate reconciliation of the reports
by the Budget and Accounting offices and at every end of the month thereafter to assure
the correctness of financial information essential in decision making.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a) Require the Municipal Treasurer immediate full settlement of the cash
advance amounting to P15,113.45;

b) Comply the requirement in the selection of person/personnel who shall
be assigned to special projects particularly in judging, technical support
and other support services which requires superior knowledge or
expertise in a specific field;

<) Refrain from granting seed money for the Festival activities
(contests/competitions) that have provisions for prizes, food allowances
and supplies, otherwise this warrant the issuance of audit disallowance;

d) Require concerned personnel assigned to each activity to prepare
detailed program of activities and submit list of items needed in a
particular activity for consolidation of the committee on the entire
festival for budget preparation; and

e) Enjoin that resources of the government shall be managed, expended or
utilized in accordance with law and regulations, and safeguard against
loss or wastage of funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
2) Send demand letters to concerned officers and employees with unliquidated cash advances requesting immediate settlement thereof; the outstanding balances were
3) Withhold the salaries due the officers and employees should they fail to settle their cash advances after the lapse of 30 days from receipt of the demand letter; 4) Require the disbursing officers to liquidate their cash advances at the end of the year in compliance to Section 5.8 of the COA Circular 97-002; 5) Reconcile the long outstanding cash advances of Ms. Lerma Bitas and Ms. Lorelei Bron totaling P17,607.73 and immediate settlement must be made thereafter. 6) We likewise recommended for Management to discontinue the practice of granting cash advance for travelling expense to one (1) official or employee and accommodate the travel expenses of other officials and employees therein.
